records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Inclosed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Inclosing.]

defs: To surround; to shut in; to confine on all sides; to include; to shut up; to encompass; as, to inclose a fort or an army with troops; to inclose a town with walls.
quotes: How many evils have inclosed me round!

defs: To put within a case, envelope, or the like; to fold (a thing) within another or into the same parcel; as, to inclose a letter or a bank note.
quotes: The inclosed copies of the treaty.

defs: To separate from common grounds by a fence; as, to inclose lands.
To put into harness; to harness.
quotes: They went to coach and their horse inclose.
